title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation · Decision Evidence Lens: Responsive Reflow
summary: The primary flow works by mouse at one viewport but loses focus, status, meaning, readability, and error recovery for changed devices and users. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 responsive reflow increment in accessible responsive product experience. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Responsive Reflow · Theory Responsive Reflow
steps.instruction:
theory case — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Responsive Reflow contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Responsive Reflow · Theory Responsive Reflow.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions. For Predict Responsive Reflow · Theory Responsive Reflow, keep Responsive Reflow connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Responsive Reflow condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub healthy contribution and repository security documentation — Controls contribution guidance, governance, licensing, support, security policy, dependency review, code scanning, secret scanning, and repository stewardship.

hints: For Predict Responsive Reflow · Theory Responsive Reflow, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Responsive Reflow precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Responsive Reflow · Theory Responsive Reflow, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.

For Predict Responsive Reflow · Theory Responsive Reflow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Responsive design means choosing three fixed screen widths.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Responsive design means choosing three fixed screen widths. That claim does not survive the predict responsive reflow evidence boundary.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The worked example variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions. A defensible solution applies Responsive Reflow, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to accessible responsive product experience.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Responsive design means choosing three fixed screen widths. This fails the Responsive Reflow contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Responsive Reflow failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Responsive design means choosing three fixed screen widths.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the responsive reflow defect evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Responsive Reflow, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Defend the Responsive Reflow model · Theory Responsive Reflow

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
